title: Tromotriche baylissii
taxonomy: Tromotriche baylissii is classified within the family Asclepiadaceae, now more commonly referred to as the Apocynaceae family due to its reclassification. This reclassification is based on more recent research and phylogenetic analysis. Tromotriche baylissii is further classified within the genus Tromotriche, which is a group of succulent plants native to South Africa. The genus Tromotriche is part of the subfamily Periplocoideae within the Apocynaceae family. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of its family.

intro: Tromotriche baylissii, also known as the "carrion flower", is a rare and unique succulent plant that obtains moisture from the air through a process called "crassulacean acid metabolism". The health benefits of Tromotriche baylissii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties.
